Package org.mose.command.arguments.operation

package org.mose.command.arguments.operation
  • Class Summary
    Class
    Description
    AnyArgument<A>
    AnyArgument is used for if you have a collection of possible acceptable arguments with one of those arguments being passed to the command system
    BlankArgument<T>
     
    ExactArgument
    The exact argument is used as a string argument where there is only one single possible argument.
    FlatRemainingArgument<T>
    The flat remaining argument is designed to get all the remaining arguments whereby the argument return a list.
    OptionalArgument<T>
    The optional argument is designed to give the user a optional argument to give more detail, this could be that the user needs to specify a online player, however this could be optional if the user is already a player, whereby the optional value would be the user player.
    RemainingArgument<T>
    Parses all the string arguments that have not be processed after the last successful parse.
    SuggestionArgument<A>
    This allows you to give extra suggestions to a command argument.
  • Record Class Summary
    Record Class
    Description
    MappedArgumentWrapper<T,​J>
    Used for mapping one argument to another.
    OptionalArgument.WrappedParser<T>
    If the Optional not provided value is known, then you can use a WrappedParser to pass the argument to the OptionalArgument if needed
